findings.justification: notice_data['message'] is echoed raw without any escaping; messages built by third-party code via the um_admin_create_notices action or um_adm_action_custom_update_notice filter can inject arbitrary HTML/JS that executes in an admin's browser.

message['content'] and message['err_content'] values returned by the apply_filters('um_adm_action_custom_update_notice') call are echoed inside

tags without sanitization, allowing filter callbacks from third-party plugins to inject arbitrary HTML/script content.

get_the_title($form_id) is concatenated raw into $content without escaping before being rendered in an admin notice, so a post title containing HTML (e.g. set by an admin or via REST API) can inject script tags into the admin panel.
_e() is called with a string containing raw HTML markup, an i18n anti-pattern that allows malicious or compromised translation files to inject HTML/script into the admin notice output.
mkdir() is called with mode 0777 (world-writable/executable) for the languages/plugins directory, which may allow other OS-level users on shared hosts to write files into that directory.

force_dismiss_notice() verifies the nonce but never checks current_user_can(), so any authenticated user who can obtain the 'um-admin-nonce' value (e.g. via another admin page) could permanently hide admin notices from the options table.

dismiss_notice() delegates capability verification to UM()->admin()->check_ajax_nonce() rather than explicitly calling current_user_can(), relying entirely on that helper function to enforce access control; if that function only validates the nonce, low-privilege authenticated users could dismiss admin notices.
